disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Mechanism: brief accidental contact with boiling water on the left volar forearm approximately 45 minutes ago
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Boiling water from the kettle splashed onto the inside of my forearm about three quarters of an hour ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: First aid already given: held the area under cool running tap water for about 15 minutes immediately after the injury
trigger_question_examples: What have you done for it so far?
Did you put anything on it or run water over it?
Have you cooled the burn at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I ran it under the cold tap for about 15 minutes straight after it happened. I haven't put any cream or anything on it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Appearance: uniform bright erythema, dry surface, NO blisters or bullae, blanches briskly and completely on light pressure — features of a superficial epidermal burn
trigger_question_examples: Are there any blisters, or is the skin broken or weeping?
Is the skin dry or is it oozing? Any blisters forming?
When you press the red area, does it go pale and then the colour come back?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blisters at all, and it's not weeping — the skin's dry, just very red. When I press it, it goes pale and then the redness comes straight back.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Size and site: patch roughly 10 x 8 cm (about one palm) on the left volar forearm — approximately 1% total body surface area
trigger_question_examples: Roughly how big is the burnt area — can you compare it to your palm?
How much of your arm is affected?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's about the size of my palm, on the soft inner side of my forearm. Just the one patch.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Safety screen negative: no facial or airway involvement, no inhalation of steam/smoke, burn does not cross a major joint or involve hands/genitals, no other injury
trigger_question_examples: Was your face, mouth or throat affected, or did you breathe in any steam?
Are your hands, or any other part of you, burnt as well?
Did the burn go across a joint like your elbow or wrist?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's only that one patch on my forearm. My face is fine, I didn't breathe in any steam, and my hands are okay. It's not near my elbow or wrist.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is exacerbated by ambient air currents moving across the exposed sensitive skin
trigger_question_examples: Is anything setting the pain off or making it sting more?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It stings much more whenever air blows over it — like when I walk past a fan or the window.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone; accidental mechanism with no ongoing safety hazard in the kitchen
trigger_question_examples: Is there anyone at home with you?
How did it happen — is everything safe in the kitchen now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own. It was just an accident — the kettle's fine, nothing dangerous set up.
